title: Sony will stop manufacturing physical discs for PlayStation in January 2028
summary: The Japanese company confirms the end of physical game production for its consoles, marking the beginning of the total digital era on PlayStation.

title: US lifts restrictions on Anthropic's AI models Fable 5 and Mythos 5
summary: Trump administration removes export controls on these models after a jailbreak, allowing their global use.

title: Anthropic introduces Claude Sonnet 5, a cheaper AI model for running agents
summary: The company launches a more affordable version of its flagship model, closing the gap with the Opus series and hiding price increases in token rates.

title: Google introduces Nano Banana 2 Lite, a fast and cheap image generator, along with the Gemini Omni Flash video model
summary: Google's new AI models allow creating images and videos more efficiently and economically via API.

title: Anthropic presents Claude Science, an AI workspace designed for scientists
summary: The tool aims to improve productivity in scientific research by integrating specialized workflows.

title: The game Cinder City sets a record by requiring 64 GB of RAM in its recommended requirements
summary: The independent title drastically raises the hardware bar needed to play, surpassing any previous standard.

title: OpenClaw launches native apps for iOS and Android
summary: The open-source platform now allows native access from mobile devices.

title: WinRAR releases version 7.23 with security fixes and improvements
summary: The popular file compressor updates its software and thanks users who finally pay for the new version.

title: South Korea plans an investment of over $500 billion alongside Samsung and SK Hynix to lead AI memory chips
summary: The South Korean government and the country's largest semiconductor companies join forces to secure their dominance in the AI memory market.

title: PS5 Linux now works on the PlayStation 5 Slim with firmware 7.61
summary: The PS5 Linux project extends compatibility to the PlayStation 5 Slim, increasing the number of supported consoles.

title: Steam Machines improve performance when using RAM in dual-channel mode
summary: Tests show that using dual-channel memory increases gaming performance, while single-channel hampers the system.

title: The NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090D HOF OC LAB becomes the first Blackwell GPU to reach 4 GHz
summary: Extreme overclocking allows the high-end graphics card to surpass the 4 GHz barrier, setting a new record.

title: NVIDIA optimizes its Blackwell GPUs for DeepSeek and reduces token cost by up to 80%
summary: The optimization allows running DeepSeek models more efficiently, drastically reducing computational costs.

title: PlayStation 6 will have a material cost close to $1,000 and Sony promises more formats and technology
summary: Early reports point to a very expensive console due to RAM shortages, while Sony prepares a strategy to win back PC gamers.

title: GTA VI and the absence of a physical disc threaten traditional video game stores
summary: The lack of a physical edition for GTA VI leads to poor pre-order numbers and puts retailers like GameStop at risk.

title: Meta builds a cloud business to sell its surplus AI compute capacity to external customers
summary: Mark Zuckerberg's company emulates SpaceX by monetizing its surplus AI infrastructure, offering cloud services to third parties.

title: Xbox suffers studio layoffs and thousands of employees unionize to protest
summary: The creators of 007 First Light announce staff cuts, while more than 3,500 Xbox workers organize against mass layoffs.

title: Sony removes 551 purchased movies and will close PS3 and PS Vita store in 2026
summary: The company continues its digital shift by removing purchased content and closing purchase platforms on old consoles.

title: Microsoft plans new job cuts and discontinues budget models Surface Go and Laptop Go
summary: The tech company adjusts its workforce and Surface product lineup, eliminating the most affordable options.

title: Bill Gates says AI will enable early retirement, while companies regret AI-driven layoffs
summary: The debate over AI's impact on employment intensifies with optimistic predictions and cases of corporate regret.
